reasoning: Based on Bitcoin's 4-year halving cycle, post-halving years (2013, 2017, 2021) have historically always ended higher than they started. Furthermore, Bitcoin has ended higher than its start in about 78% of its trading years overall (11 out of 14 years). The starting price of $93,429.20 is already quite high, and there is a structural risk of cycle left-translation—where the market peaks early in the year and crashes before the end of December. However, this is offset by strong bullish catalysts, such as the explicitly pro-crypto stance of the Trump administration (as noted in the background text) and continued institutional adoption. Balancing the strong base rates for a post-halving year with the potential downside volatility from a high base price, I assess a 68% probability that Bitcoin will end 2025 higher than it started.
